01-01: If New Year's Eve wind blows south, it betokeneth warmth and growth.
01-06: If Twelfth Night be windy, a good year for corn.
01-13: St. Hilary's Day is said to be the coldest day of the year.
01-20: St. Agnes' Day fair means a good year for lambs.
01-25: If it rains on St. Paul's Day, it will be a bad year.
02-02: If Candlemas Day be fair and bright, winter will have another flight. If on Candlemas Day it showers and rains, winter is gone and will not come again.
02-03: St. Blaise's wind predicts the weather for the next forty days.
02-14: If St. Valentine's Day is fair, spring will come early.
02-22: St. Peter's Chair: if sunny, spring is near.
02-24: St. Matthias breaks the ice; if there is none, he makes it.
03-01: If it rains on St. David's Day, the whole spring will be wet.
03-12: If it rains on St. Gregory's Day, the summer will be wet.
03-17: St. Patrick's Day cold and wet, summer's rains will be early set.
03-19: St. Joseph's Day fine means a warm spring to follow.
03-25: If it rains on Lady Day, the summer will be dry.
04-04: If it rains on St. Ambrose's Day, the summer will be cool.
04-14: St. Tiburtius Day: if fine, summer will be pleasant.
04-23: St. George's Day fair weather foretells a plentiful year.
04-25: If it rains on St. Mark's Day, it will rain for forty days.
05-01: A cold May is kindly, and fills the barn finely.
05-11: Mamertus, Pancras, Gervase – beware the ice saints' days.
05-12: St. Pancras: if cold, summer will be short.
05-25: Urban's Day sunshine promises a rich grape harvest.
06-08: If it rains on St. Medard's Day, it will rain for forty days.
06-24: If it rains on St. John's Day, the harvest will be poor.
06-29: If it rains on SS. Peter and Paul's Day, there will be a poor harvest.
07-02: If it rains on the Visitation of Our Lady, the wine harvest will fail.
07-15: St. Swithin's Day, if thou dost rain, for forty days it will remain. St. Swithin's Day, if thou be fair, for forty days 'twill rain no more.
07-20: If it thunders on St. Elijah's Day, an early autumn is expected.
07-22: Tears of St. Mary Magdalene – if it rains, the autumn will be wet.
07-25: If it rains on St. James' Day, the autumn will be wet.
07-26: St. Anne's rain means a good wine year.
08-01: Lammas Day: if it rains, the autumn will be wet.
08-10: If it rains on St. Lawrence's Day, the autumn will be wet.
08-15: If it rains on the Assumption of Our Lady, the autumn will be poor.
08-24: If it rains on St. Bartholomew's Day, it rains the whole autumn through.
09-08: If it is fine at Our Lady's Birthday, expect a mild winter.
09-14: Holy Cross Day: fair weather means a long warm autumn.
09-21: St. Matthew brings cold rain and dew.
09-29: Michaelmas Day fine and clear brings a long and pleasant autumn here.
10-04: St. Francis' Day rain means a rainy October.
10-18: St. Luke's Day: sow wheat, for the saint blesses it.
10-28: If St. Simon and Jude's Day be fine, winter arrives soon.
11-01: All Saints' Day fine means a mild winter.
11-11: If Martinmas be fine and clear, look for frosty winters here.
11-19: St. Elizabeth's snow foretells a heavy winter.
11-25: If St. Catherine frowns with white, Christmas will be light.
11-30: If it rains on St. Andrew's Day, Christmas will be white.
12-04: St. Barbara's snow melts by Christmas.
12-06: St. Nicholas' frost means a green Christmas.
12-13: As the weather on St. Lucy's Day, so the winter will stay.
12-21: St. Thomas' Day cold means a hard winter ahead.
12-25: A green Christmas makes a fat graveyard.
12-26: If it rains on St. Stephen's Day, the next year's harvest will be good.
12-31: If New Year's Eve night wind blows south, it betokeneth warmth and growth.
